question.text: An insider traded in securities based on unpublished price-sensitive information and made profits of Rs. 12 crore. What is the maximum penalty that can be imposed under Section 15G of the SEBI Act, 1992?

answer.text: Rs. 36 crore (three times the profits made)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 15G, the penalty for insider trading can extend to twenty-five crore rupees or three times the amount of profits made out of insider trading, whichever is higher. Here, 3 x Rs. 12 crore = Rs. 36 crore, which exceeds Rs. 25 crore, so the maximum penalty is Rs. 36 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 25 crore is the fixed upper limit but only applies when it is higher than three times the profit. In this case, three times the profit exceeds Rs. 25 crore. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) and NISM Series VIII cover insider trading penalties. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The penalty for insider trading is designed to be punitive - always ensure compliance with insider trading norms as penalties can far exceed the gains made.
